channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Talking to Yourself: Defying Forgetting in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2602.20162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Catastrophic forgetting remains a major challenge when fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) on narrow, task-specific data, often degrading their general knowledge and reasoning abilities. We propose SA-SFT, a lightweight self-augmentation routine ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, alignment, reasoning, analysis, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ConceptRM: The Quest to Mitigate Alert Fatigue through Consensus-Based Purity-Driven Data Cleaning for Reflection Modelling
item.description: arXiv:2602.20166v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In many applications involving intelligent agents, the overwhelming volume of alerts (mostly false) generated by the agents may desensitize users and cause them to overlook critical issues, leading to the so-called ''alert fatigue''. A common strategy...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, product, LLM, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: InterviewSim: A Scalable Framework for Interview-Grounded Personality Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2602.20294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Simulating real personalities with large language models requires grounding generation in authentic personal data. Existing evaluation approaches rely on demographic surveys, personality questionnaires, or short AI-led interviews as proxies, but lack ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, alignment, retrieval, augmented, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: What Makes a Good Query? Measuring the Impact of Human-Confusing Linguistic Features on LLM Performance
item.description: arXiv:2602.20300v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) hallucinations are usually treated as defects of the model or its decoding strategy. Drawing on classical linguistics, we argue that a query's form can also shape a listener's (and model's) response. We operationalize this i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, vector, analysis, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
item.description: arXiv:2602.20332v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Advanced reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs) have led to more frequent hallucinations; yet most mitigation work focuses on open-source models for post-hoc detection and parameter editing. The dearth of studies focusing on hallucinat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, context, zero-shot, reasoning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Natural Language Processing Models for Robust Document Categorization
item.description: arXiv:2602.20336v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This article presents an evaluation of several machine learning methods applied to automated text classification, alongside the design of a demonstrative system for unbalanced document categorization and distribution. The study focuses on balancing cl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, context, RAG, ICL, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How communicatively optimal are exact numeral systems? Once more on lexicon size and morphosyntactic complexity
item.description: arXiv:2602.20372v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent research argues that exact recursive numeral systems optimize communicative efficiency by balancing a tradeoff between the size of the numeral lexicon and the average morphosyntactic complexity (roughly length in morphemes) of numeral terms. We...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, research, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Case-Aware LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation for Enterprise-Scale RAG Systems
item.description: arXiv:2602.20379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enterprise Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) assistants operate in multi-turn, case-based workflows such as technical support and IT operations, where evaluation must reflect operational constraints, structured identifiers (e.g., error codes, versi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, instruction, retrieval, product, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Disentangling Geometry, Performance, and Training in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2602.20433v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geometric properties of Transformer weights, particularly the unembedding matrix, have been widely useful in language model interpretability research. Yet, their utility for estimating downstream performance remains unclear. In this work, we systemati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, embedding, analysis, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DMCD: Semantic-Statistical Framework for Causal Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2602.20333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present DMCD (DataMap Causal Discovery), a two-phase causal discovery framework that integrates LLM-based semantic drafting from variable metadata with statistical validation on observational data. In Phase I, a large language model proposes a spar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, analysis, framework, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Implicit Intelligence -- Evaluating Agents on What Users Don't Say
item.description: arXiv:2602.20424v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world requests to AI agents are fundamentally underspecified. Natural human communication relies on shared context and unstated constraints that speakers expect listeners to infer. Current agentic benchmarks test explicit instruction-following bu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, instruction, reasoning, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning to Rewrite Tool Descriptions for Reliable LLM-Agent Tool Use
item.description: arXiv:2602.20426v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The performance of LLM-based agents depends not only on the agent itself but also on the quality of the tool interfaces it consumes. While prior work has focused heavily on agent fine-tuning, tool interfaces-including natural language descriptions and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG, vision, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PreScience: A Benchmark for Forecasting Scientific Contributions
item.description: arXiv:2602.20459v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Can AI systems trained on the scientific record up to a fixed point in time forecast the scientific advances that follow? Such a capability could help researchers identify collaborators and impactful research directions, and anticipate which problems ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, paper, product, RAG, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: KairosVL: Orchestrating Time Series and Semantics for Unified Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2602.20494v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Driven by the increasingly complex and decision-oriented demands of time series analysis, we introduce the Semantic-Conditional Time Series Reasoning task, which extends conventional time series analysis beyond purely numerical modeling to incorporate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, analysis, reasoning, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ActionEngine: From Reactive to Programmatic GUI Agents via State Machine Memory
item.description: arXiv:2602.20502v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents operate through step-by-step calls to vision language models--taking a screenshot, reasoning about the next action, executing it, then repeating on the new page--resulting in high costs and latency that s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> step-by-step, reasoning, RAG, vision, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Inner Speech as Behavior Guides: Steerable Imitation of Diverse Behaviors for Human-AI coordination
item.description: arXiv:2602.20517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effective human-AI coordination requires artificial agents capable of exhibiting and responding to human-like behaviors while adapting to changing contexts. Imitation learning has emerged as one of the prominent approaches to build such agents by trai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, demonstration, framework, vision, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MoBiQuant: Mixture-of-Bits Quantization for Token-Adaptive Elastic LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2602.20191v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Changing runtime complexity on cloud and edge devices necessitates elastic large language model (LLM) deployment, where an LLM can be inferred with various quantization precisions based on available computational resources. However, it has been observ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, framework, LLM, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FedAvg-Based CTMC Hazard Model for Federated Bridge Deterioration Assessment
item.description: arXiv:2602.20194v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bridge periodic inspection records contain sensitive information about public infrastructure, making cross-organizational data sharing impractical under existing data governance constraints. We propose a federated framework for estimating a Continuous...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, platform, vector, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Controllable Exploration in Hybrid-Policy RLVR for Multi-Modal Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2602.20197v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a primary learning paradigm for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of multi-modal large language models (MLLMs). However, during RL training, the enormous state space of MLLM and s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, paper, product, reasoning, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Golden Layers and Where to Find Them: Improved Knowledge Editing for Large Language Models Via Layer Gradient Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2602.20207v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge editing in Large Language Models (LLMs) aims to update the model's prediction for a specific query to a desired target while preserving its behavior on all other inputs. This process typically involves two stages: identifying the layer to ed...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, analysis, LLM, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Model Merging in the Essential Subspace
item.description: arXiv:2602.20208v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Model merging aims to integrate multiple task-specific fine-tuned models derived from a shared pre-trained checkpoint into a single multi-task model without additional training. Despite extensive research, task interference remains a major obstacle th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, analysis, framework, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: KnapSpec: Self-Speculative Decoding via Adaptive Layer Selection as a Knapsack Problem
item.description: arXiv:2602.20217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-speculative decoding (SSD) accelerates LLM inference by skipping layers to create an efficient draft model, yet existing methods often rely on static heuristics that ignore the dynamic computational overhead of attention in long-context scenarios...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, attention, analysis, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MultiModalPFN: Extending Prior-Data Fitted Networks for Multimodal Tabular Learning
item.description: arXiv:2602.20223v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recently, TabPFN has gained attention as a foundation model for tabular data. However, it struggles to integrate heterogeneous modalities such as images and text, which are common in domains like healthcare and marketing, thereby limiting its applicab...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, attention, embedding, image, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt engineering, prompt, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, audio, framework, chain-of-thought, CoT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, retrieval, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, retrieval, reasoning, knowledge base, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, vector, framework, LLM, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: Multimodal Crystal Flow: Any-to-Any Modality Generation for Unified Crystal Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2602.20210v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Crystal modeling spans a family of conditional and unconditional generation tasks across different modalities, including crystal structure prediction (CSP) and \emph{de novo} generation (DNG). While recent deep generative models have shown promising p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, framework, multimodal, template, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Benchmarking Distilled Language Models: Performance and Efficiency in Resource-Constrained Settings
item.description: arXiv:2602.20164v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge distillation offers a transformative pathway to developing powerful, yet efficient, small language models (SLMs) suitable for resource-constrained environments. In this paper, we benchmark the performance and computational cost of distilled ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, paper, reasoning, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An artificial intelligence framework for end-to-end rare disease phenotyping from clinical notes using large language models
item.description: arXiv:2602.20324v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Phenotyping is fundamental to rare disease diagnosis, but manual curation of structured phenotypes from clinical notes is labor-intensive and difficult to scale. Existing artificial intelligence approaches typically optimize individual components of p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> step-by-step, prompt engineering, example, prompt, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.description: arXiv:2602.20422v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion models have shown promising capabilities in trajectory generation for planning in offline reinforcement learning (RL). item.title: Tensor Network Generator-Enhanced Optimization for Traveling Salesman Problem
item.description: arXiv:2602.20175v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present an application of the tensor network generator-enhanced optimization (TN-GEO) framework to address the traveling salesman problem (TSP), a fundamental combinatorial optimization challenge.
